task: Remove the bubble ring from the assembled bubble ring and ball
raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS - A blue ball and a yellow ball are on a table. The blue ball is a larger, matte sphere; the yellow ball is smaller and has a shiny surface. They are in contact, forming an assembled bubble ring. STRATEGY - First, grasp the yellow ball (the smaller one) to separate it from the blue ball. Then, transport and place the yellow ball onto the blue ball to reassemble the bubble ring. Failure modes include dropping the ball during transport or misplacing it. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1: Reach for the yellow ball's body to grasp it. Step 2: Grasp the yellow ball's body for a secure hold. Step 3: Transport the yellow ball to the blue ball. Step 4: Place the yellow ball on top of the blue ball.**
Goal: stacked({yellow_ball, blue_ball})
Scene: blue_ball, yellow_ball, table
Step 1: reach(yellow_ball)
  affordance_region: the body of the yellow ball
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, yellow_ball, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, yellow_ball) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(yellow_ball)
  affordance_region: the body of the yellow ball
  contact: holding(yellow_ball) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(yellow_ball)
Step 3: transport(yellow_ball -> blue_ball)
  affordance_region: the top surface of the blue ball
  spatial: above(yellow_ball, blue_ball, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(yellow_ball)
  done_when: above(yellow_ball, blue_ball, 0.05)
Step 4: place(yellow_ball -> blue_ball)
  affordance_region: the top surface of the blue ball
  contact: surface_contact(yellow_ball, blue_ball) [completion]; released(yellow_ball) [completion]
  pose: stacked({yellow_ball, blue_ball}) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(yellow_ball, blue_ball) AND released(yellow_ball)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the yellow ball
the body of the yellow ball
the top surface of the blue ball
the top surface of the blue ball
